dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 14, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**MLB Recap: Thursday's Action**

Thursday's MLB slate featured a mix of high-scoring affairs and dominant pitching performances. In the Arizona Diamondbacks' 4-3 win over the Baltimore Orioles, Ildemaro Vargas went deep twice, driving in three runs, while Merrill Kelly earned the win in relief. On the mound, Trevor Rogers struggled, allowing three runs over 4.2 innings for the Orioles.

In the Detroit Tigers' 2-1 victory over the Kansas City Royals, Dillon Dingler doubled and scored the go-ahead run in the seventh inning. Will Vest earned the win with a scoreless ninth inning, striking out three Royals.

The Washington Nationals outlasted the Pittsburgh Pirates 5-4, behind CJ Abrams' three-hit, two-RBI performance. Mitchell Parker tossed two scoreless innings for the win, striking out five Pirates.

Thursday's action also saw the Los Angeles Angels dominate the New York Yankees 7-1, with Reid Detmers allowing just one earned run over seven innings. Yoán Moncada homered and drove in three runs for the Angels.

Byron Buxton continued his hot streak, homering twice and driving in two runs in the Minnesota Twins' 6-0 win over the Boston Red Sox. Mick Abel pitched a complete-game shutout, striking out 10 Red Sox.

The Tampa Bay Rays edged the Chicago White Sox 8-5, behind Shane McClanahan's five scoreless innings and Everson Pereira's three-RBI performance.

In other results, the Toronto Blue Jays rallied for a 9-7 win over the Milwaukee Brewers, while the Atlanta Braves outlasted the Miami Marlins 6-5. The St. Louis Cardinals edged the Cleveland Guardians 6-5, and the Houston Astros came from behind to beat the Colorado Rockies 7-6.
